issues_found.description: EXP-052 is missing from monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json while EXP-051, EXP-053, EXP-054 exist. next_id invariant still holds (55>54), so next allocation is safe, but the gap should be investigated to confirm it's intentional rather than a lost tracker entry.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should check git history for any commit that touched EXP-052 and confirm whether it was deliberately skipped or silently dropped by a concurrent write. If intentional, document the skip; if dropped, restore from the orphaned expansions/*.json files.

summary: Structural health is green. Test suite (2242 cases) passes; build is reproducible; data↔prose counts match; discoverability infrastructure (llms.txt, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, uncounted-failures.json, meta tags) is intact; expansion tracker next_id invariant holds; priority-queue coupling is correct; no workspace-sync direction violations. The one warn is a single ID gap at EXP-052 in the expansion tracker — cosmetic and non-blocking, but worth a decider investigation to confirm it's intentional.
